evidence: "True to Life, Painful, Brave, Nuanced, and Suffused with the Sadness of Missed Chances"
"The characters are complex and flawed and one's view of them changes constantly..."
"The story was multi-layered. Many times I expected some violent incident but I was immensely relieved that the film never resorted to this type of a device."
"The way in which the director leads one from one's initial perceptions... to the revelation of the details of their relationship is artful."
"The film captures the chess world (male dominated, intimidating, turned inward) well although it is not credible that the protagonist could become so good..."
"The acting by Ana Torrent is the best acting work by a child that I have ever seen."
"I love Agatha Christie and had read many of her books, but not this one."
"I loved Archipelago and Unrelated but this was just awful."
"The Swiss mountains are rendered so perfectly that you will feel cold watching the film."
"The film is ultimately haunting, affecting and true-to-life."

summary: The user has a strong preference for complex dramas and documentaries, particularly those with nuanced characterizations and emotional depth. They appreciate high production quality and sensory experiences, often favoring slow-paced narratives that allow for character development and thematic exploration.
